initial_diagnostic_question.question: Does this ECG suggest the presence of anterior ischemia?

criterion_selection.question: To accurately diagnose anterior ischemia, which of the following diagnostic criteria should be evaluated?
options: Presence of ST-segment elevation in anterior leads
Presence of ST-segment depression in anterior leads
Presence of ST-segment depression in leads II, III, and aVF
Presence of J-point elevation in anterior leads
criterion_selection.answer: Presence of ST-segment depression in anterior leads

finding_identification.question: Regarding the criterion you selected, does this ECG show ST-segment depression in at least two of the anterior leads, including leads V2, V3, and V4? Note that ST depression is defined as a depression of the J-point greater than 0.1mV (1mm) in lead V2, and greater than 0.07mV (0.7mm) in leads V3 and V4.

ecg_grounding.question: Which of the following leads show ST-segment depression? Select all possible leads from the options below.
Within the selected leads, in which of the following waves can you observe the QRS complex with ST-segment depression? The options below refer to time ranges on the ECG signal, provided in seconds.

diagnostic_decision.question: Based on the finding identified above, does this ECG suggest the presence of anterior ischemia?
options: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis
